phrases.intent_en: Ask for directions to the abbey entrance from the main street
phrases.text_local: Par où monte-t-on vers l'entrée de l'abbaye ?
phrases.pronunciation: par oo MOHNT-tohn vair lahn-TRAY duh lab-BAY-ee
phrases.cultural_note: The Grande Rue is the single main street climbing through the village to the abbey. It is lined with souvenir shops, crêperies and restaurants — the entrance staircase is at the top.

responses.english: Straight ahead, climb to the top.

phrases.intent_en: Buy a jar of local salted butter caramel
phrases.text_local: Avez-vous du caramel au beurre salé de Normandie ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah-vay-VOO doo kah-rah-MEL oh BUR sah-LAY duh nor-mahn-DEE
phrases.cultural_note: Salted butter caramel is a Normandy speciality. Shops on the Grande Rue sell it in jars — an excellent and practical edible souvenir.

responses.english: Yes, the jars are on the shelf.
responses.phonetic: wee lay POH sohn soor lay-tah-ZHAIR
responses.local: Oui, les pots sont sur l'étagère.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how long the climb to the abbey takes
phrases.text_local: Combien de temps faut-il pour monter jusqu'à l'abbaye à pied ?
phrases.pronunciation: kohm-BYAHN duh tahn foh-TEEL poor mohn-TAY zhoos-kah lab-BAY-ee ah PYAY
phrases.cultural_note: The climb from the main gate to the abbey entrance takes roughly 15–20 minutes on foot. The Grande Rue can be very crowded in summer — start early in the morning.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if a shop sells local Norman products
phrases.text_local: Est-ce que vous vendez des produits régionaux normands ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess kuh voo vahn-DAY day proh-DWEE ray-zhyoh-NOH nor-MAHN
phrases.cultural_note: Look for Calvados apple brandy, cidre normand, and camembert alongside the caramel. Many shops on the Grande Rue specialise in regional Norman food products.

responses.english: Yes, we have cider and Camembert.
responses.phonetic: wee ohn ah doo SEE-druh ay doo kah-mahm-BAIR
responses.local: Oui, on a du cidre et du camembert.

phrases.intent_en: Ask a shopkeeper for a bag
phrases.text_local: Pourriez-vous me donner un sac, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: poor-YAY-voo muh doh-NAY uhn SAK seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: Single-use plastic bags are banned in France. Shops typically offer paper bags or ask you to bring your own reusable tote.

responses.phonetic: tuh-NAY vwah-LAH uhn SAK ahn pah-PYAY
responses.local: Tenez, voilà un sac en papier.
